__comment: EuroTransit order trace dashboard — paste an order ID, find the trace whose Orders request span carries span.order.id, select the result, and render the complete trace traversal in an embedded Grafana traces panel.
title: EuroTransit — Trace an order

list.description: Paste the orderId returned by POST /orders.
list.label: Order ID
list.name: order_id

list.description: Set by the search-result link; drives the trace waterfall.
list.label: Trace ID
list.name: trace_id

panels.title: Follow one order through the money path

options.content:
### 1. Paste the order ID above · 2. Select the matching row · 3. Read the waterfall below
The expected happy path is **Orders accept → `order-placed` → Inventory reserve → `inventory-reserved` → Orders call Payments → `payment-authorized` → Orders confirm → `order-confirmed` → Notifications**.

The lookup uses the high-cardinality Tempo span attribute `order.id`; it is deliberately **not** a Prometheus label. Widen the dashboard time range if the order is older than the current 6-hour view; Tempo currently retains 48 hours. Newly accepted traces can continue receiving Kafka spans after the HTTP root span has ended, so refresh while an order is still progressing.

panels.title: Trace matching order $order_id
panels.description: The Orders checkout span is tagged with order.id once the generated or idempotently replayed ID is known. Select any cell and choose 'Show trace path below' to populate the embedded waterfall.

panels.title: Order $order_id — complete trace path
panels.description: Full trace traversal for the selected trace ID. Expand spans to inspect service, Kafka, HTTP, duration, status, and attributes; use the critical-path control to highlight the latency-dominant chain.
